mission.title: Install and Configure Kubernetes on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open-source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts. It provides essential mechanisms for the deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications, making it a critical tool for modern cloud-native environments.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Helm chart repository for Kubernetes.
```bash
helm repo add kvaps https://kvaps.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kubernetes using Helm
steps.description:
Install the Kubernetes Helm chart in the 'kube-system' namespace with the specified version.
```bash
helm install kubernetes kvaps/kubernetes --version 0.13.5 --namespace kube-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Kubernetes pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kube-system
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Kubernetes deployment to ensure efficient resource usage.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/kubernetes --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --namespace kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Kubernetes Helm release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall kubernetes --namespace kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and other resources associated with the installation.
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'kube-system' namespace if no other resources are using it.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kube-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and state of the Kubernetes deployment.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kube-system -o yaml > kube-system-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes installation
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Kubernetes Helm release to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade kubernetes kvaps/kubernetes --namespace kube-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded Kubernetes pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kube-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue occurs when a pod fails to start repeatedly. To diagnose, check the pod logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kube-system
``` 
Fix by reviewing the logs for errors and adjusting the configuration as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating that the Helm release cannot be found, ensure that you are in the correct namespace:
```bash
helm list --namespace kube-system
``` 
If the release is not listed, verify the installation step.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are not scheduling due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kube-system
``` 
Adjust the resource limits as needed.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kubernetes will have all pods in the 'kube-system' namespace running without errors. You can verify this by running 'kubectl get pods --namespace kube-system' and ensuring all pods are in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kvaps https://kvaps.github.io/charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl.
